RULING - NOTICE OF INTENT TO APPEAR TO ARGUE THE TENTATIVE RULING WAS NOT GIVEN. TENTATIVE RULING WAS ADOPTED. Plaintiff George Kulya's Motion To Augment Expert Witness Information is denied. The court does not find that the failure to designate an forensic document examiner was the result of mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT =612/EAH

Ntc Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Further Response To Demand For Production Of Docs
Off Calendar Jul-02-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearance:
Natalia N. Litchev, Esq., for Gennady Yeltchev, Defendant/Moving Party
Albert Z. Gelin, Esq., for George Kulya, Plaintiff/Opposing Party

Hearing held and Tentative Ruling Adopted: Defendant Gennady Yeltchev's Motion To Compel Further Response To Demand For Production Of Documents, Special Interrogatories, Form Interrogatories is granted in part and denied in part.
The motion is denied as to Demand For Production Of Documents to Kulya Numbers 11 and 17.
The motion is granted as to Special Interrogatories to Kulya Numbers 15, 19, and 20.
The motion is granted as to Form Interrogatories to Kulya No. 12.1.
The motion is denied as to Demand for Documents to Citybest Numbers 10 and 13.
The motion is granted as to Special Interrogatories to Citybest Numbers 15 and 18.
The motion is granted as to Form Interrogatories to Citybest No. 12.1.
Further responses shall be made by July 6, 2007.
All requests for sanctions are denied. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
